content:
2 min read Just now -- Press enter or click to view image in full size Angular has been evolving rapidly in the last few versions. One of the biggest improvements is the introduction of Signals, which provide a simpler and more efficient way to manage state. With Angular 22, Signal Forms have become more stable and powerful, giving developers a modern alternative to Reactive Forms and Template-Driven Forms. In this article, we’ll understand: • What are Signal Forms? • Why Angular introduced them? • How to use Signal Forms? • How Signal Form are different than Reactive Forms What Are Signal Forms? Signal Forms are Angular forms built using Angular Signals. Instead of using, FormControl, FormGroup, valueChanges, subscriptions, we use Signals to track and update form values automatically. The main goal is: • Less boilerplate code • Better performance • Simpler state management • More reactive UI updates Why Did Angular Introduce Signal Forms? Traditional Reactive Forms work well, but they have some challenges: Reactive Forms Problems this.userForm = new FormGroup({ name: new FormControl(''), email: new FormControl('') });this.userForm.get('name')?.valueChanges.subscribe(value => { console.log(value); }); Issues: • Too much boilerplate • Manual subscriptions • Need to unsubscribe sometimes • Complex for large forms • Change detection runs more often Angular Signals solve these problems by making updates automatic and more efficient. Creating Your First Signal Form Let’s create a simple user registration form. Component import { Component, signal } from '@angular/core';@Component({ selector: 'app-user', templateUrl: './user.component.html' }) export class UserComponent { user = signal({ name: '', email: '' }); updateName(event: Event) { const value = (event.target as HTMLInputElement).value; this.user.update(user => ({ ...user, name: value })); } submit() { console.log(this.user()); } } Template

Name: {{ user().name }}

Whenever the value changes, Angular automatically updates the UI. No subscriptions. No valueChanges. No FormControl. Reactive Forms vs Signal Forms Press enter or click to view image in full size Conclusion Signal Forms represent the next generation of form handling in Angular. Reactive Forms are not going away anytime soon, but for new Angular 22 applications, Signal Forms are becoming the recommended approach. If you’re starting a new project today, it’s worth learning and adopting Signal Forms because they align perfectly with Angular’s future direction.
